class TestURLIncrementBug3EncodedDataLoss:
    """
    Bug 3: URL-encoded data is lost.

    When performing increment/decrement operations, percent-encoded characters
    are sometimes decoded/lost, breaking the URL.

    Expected: http://example.com/hello%20world1 incremented -> http://example.com/hello%20world2
    Bug: The %20 is decoded to a space or lost, producing invalid URLs
    """

    def test_space_encoding_preserved_increment(self):
        """Test that %20 (space) encoding is preserved during increment."""
        url_path = "/hello%20world1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        # %20 must be preserved, not decoded to a literal space
        assert "%20" in result, f"Space encoding %20 was lost in {result}"
        assert " " not in result, f"Literal space found where %20 should be in {result}"
        assert result == "/hello%20world2"

    def test_tab_encoding_preserved(self):
        """Test that %09 (tab) encoding is preserved."""
        url_path = "/data%091"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%09" in result, f"Tab encoding %09 was lost in {result}"
        assert "\t" not in result, f"Literal tab found where %09 should be in {result}"

    def test_newline_encoding_preserved(self):
        """Test that %0A (newline) encoding is preserved."""
        url_path = "/data%0A2"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%0A" in result, f"Newline encoding %0A was lost in {result}"
        assert "\n" not in result, f"Literal newline found where %0A should be in {result}"

    def test_multiple_encoded_spaces_preserved(self):
        """Test URL with multiple encoded spaces."""
        url_path = "/hello%20world%20test1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert result.count("%20") == 2, f"Space encodings were lost in {result}"
        assert " " not in result, f"Literal spaces found in {result}"

    def test_utf8_percent_encoding_preserved(self):
        """Test that UTF-8 percent-encoded sequences are preserved."""
        # %C3%B6 is the UTF-8 encoding of ö (o-umlaut)
        url_path = "/caf%C3%B63"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%C3%B6" in result, f"UTF-8 encoding was lost in {result}"
        assert "ö" not in result, f"Encoding was decoded to literal character in {result}"
        assert result == "/caf%C3%B64"

    def test_mixed_encoded_and_literal(self):
        """Test URL mixing encoded sequences with literal characters."""
        url_path = "/path/to%20file1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%20" in result, f"Encoding was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/path/to%20file2"
arguments.new_string:
class TestURLIncrementBug3EncodedDataLoss:
    """
    Bug 3: URL-encoded data is lost.

    When performing increment/decrement operations, percent-encoded characters
    in non-digit parts of the URL are sometimes decoded or lost, breaking the URL.

    This can happen when the getter uses PrettyDecoded mode and the setter doesn't
    properly re-encode the data.

    Key issue: The regex correctly avoids matching digits in %XX sequences,
    but the URL library's handling of encoded data during get/set operations
    can cause data loss.
    """

    def test_space_encoding_preserved_increment(self):
        """Test that %20 (space) encoding is preserved during increment."""
        # Space encoding appears BEFORE the number, not in the number itself
        url_path = "/hello%20world1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        # %20 must be preserved in the result
        assert "%20" in result, f"Space encoding %20 was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/hello%20world2"

    def test_newline_encoding_preserved(self):
        """Test that %0A (newline) encoding is preserved."""
        # Newline encoding appears BEFORE the number
        url_path = "/data%0A2"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%0A" in result, f"Newline encoding %0A was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/data%0A3"

    def test_multiple_encoded_sequences_preserved(self):
        """Test URL with multiple different encoded sequences."""
        # Multiple %XX sequences should all be preserved
        url_path = "/hello%20world%20test1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert result.count("%20") == 2, f"Space encodings were lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/hello%20world%20test2"

    def test_mixed_encoded_and_literal(self):
        """Test URL mixing encoded sequences with literal characters."""
        url_path = "/path/to%20file1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%20" in result, f"Encoding was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/path/to%20file2"

    def test_encoded_percent_sign_preserved(self):
        """Test that %25 (percent sign itself) is preserved."""
        # %25 is the encoding for the percent sign
        url_path = "/data%251"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%25" in result, f"Percent encoding %25 was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/data%252"

def test_decrement_by_equal_count_raises_error(self):
        """Test that decrementing 1 by 1 raises an error (would result in 0, still invalid)."""
        url_path = "/page_1"

        # Attempting to decrement 1 by 1 should also raise an error
        with pytest.raises(IncDecError) as exc_info:
            incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'decrement', count=1)
arguments.new_string:
def test_decrement_by_equal_count_succeeds(self):
        """Test that decrementing to zero is allowed."""
        url_path = "/page_1"

        # Decrementing 1 by 1 should succeed and result in 0
        # (Zero is allowed, only negative values are disallowed)
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'decrement', count=1)
        assert result == "/page_0"

arguments.old_string:
def test_negative_result_detection(self):
        """Test that we catch the bug if negative values are produced."""
        url_path = "/page_1"

        try:
            result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'decrement', count=2)
            # If we get here, the bug is present - negative value was allowed
            assert "-" not in result, f"Bug detected: negative value allowed in {result}"
            assert "page_-1" not in result, f"Bug detected: page_-1 was produced instead of error"
        except IncDecError:
            # This is the correct behavior
            pass
arguments.new_string:
def test_no_negative_results_allowed(self):
        """Test that negative values are never produced as results."""
        # This test verifies that if an error is NOT raised, at least
        # the result doesn't contain negative values
        url_path = "/page_0"

        try:
            result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'decrement', count=1)
            # If this doesn't raise, check that we at least don't get negative values
            # This catches the bug if the validation check gets bypassed
            assert "-" not in result, f"Bug: negative value in result: {result}"
            assert "page_-" not in result, f"Bug: negative page number in result: {result}"
            # If we get here without an exception, the value should have been rejected
            assert False, "Should have raised an error for negative result"
        except IncDecError:
            # This is the correct behavior
            pass

class TestBugWithQueryAndAnchorSegments:
    """
    Tests specifically for bugs in query string and anchor/fragment handling.

    These segments are more prone to encoding issues because they often contain
    special characters that are encoded.
    """

    def test_encoded_in_query_with_number_after(self):
        """Test encoded sequence in query string followed by a number."""
        # In a real URL: http://example.com/?v=%3A5
        # The query part would be: v=%3A5
        # We should only increment 5, not touch %3A
        query_part = "v=%3A5"
        result = incdec_number_simple(query_part, 'increment')

        # The number 5 should become 6, %3A should remain unchanged
        assert "%3A" in result, f"Encoded %3A was corrupted: {result}"
        assert result == "v=%3A6"

    def test_encoded_in_anchor_with_number(self):
        """Test encoded sequence in URL anchor followed by a number."""
        # In a real URL: http://example.com/#%3A5
        # The anchor part would be: %3A5
        anchor_part = "%3A5"
        result = incdec_number_simple(anchor_part, 'increment')

        assert "%3A" in result, f"Encoded %3A was corrupted: {result}"
        assert result == "%3A6"

    def test_query_with_multiple_params_and_number(self):
        """Test query string with multiple parameters where one has a number."""
        query_part = "foo=bar%20test&page=1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(query_part, 'increment')

        # Should increment the last number (1) and preserve %20
        assert "%20" in result, f"Space encoding was lost: {result}"
        assert result == "foo=bar%20test&page=2"

    def test_encoded_ampersand_in_query(self):
        """Test that %26 (ampersand) in query string is preserved."""
        # %26 is the encoding for & in query strings
        query_part = "value=test%261&page=1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(query_part, 'increment')

        assert "%26" in result, f"Ampersand encoding was lost: {result}"
        assert result.count("%26") == 1, f"Multiple %26 sequences were affected: {result}"

arguments.new_string:
def test_encoded_percent_sign_preserved(self):
        """Test that %25 (percent sign itself) is preserved."""
        # %25 is the encoding for the percent sign
        # The number needs to come after the full %25 sequence
        url_path = "/data%25_1"
        result = incdec_number_simple(url_path, 'increment')

        assert "%25" in result, f"Percent encoding %25 was lost in {result}"
        assert result == "/data%25_2"
